hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/TM5/TM6, while avoiding off-target SSTR3/5 interactions. This will improve the selectivity margin by increasing SSTR2 affinity and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design protocol is generating candidates with strong ddG but poor structural quality. Tightening clash penalties and increasing diversity through higher candidate counts should improve structural validity without compromising selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Tightening the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and reduce structural failures due to poor packing or steric clashes.
Increasing the number of candidates will improve diversity and increase the chances of generating a structurally valid and selective design.
